symptoms.symptom_name: 5-year history of recurrent painful deep nodules in the bilateral axillae and groin with an acute coalescing flare of the right axilla

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic, recurrent, painful deep nodules confined to the apocrine-bearing flexural friction zones (axillae and groin) over years is the defining history and distribution of hidradenitis suppurativa.
A recurrent, multi-year, bilateral flexural pattern is not a one-off primary boil, which is typically a single de novo lesion without this chronic recurrent history.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic sinus tracts (tunnels), hypertrophic scarring and paired double comedones with recurrent spontaneous rupture and malodorous purulent discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Interconnected epithelialised sinus tracts, rope-like hypertrophic scarring and bridged double comedones are near-defining features of established (Hurley II/III) hidradenitis suppurativa.
A solitary ruptured epidermoid cyst does not produce chronic interconnected sinus tracts or bridged double comedones across a recurrent flexural distribution.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe uncontrolled acute pain restricting all arm movement and preventing sleep and dressing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A severe HS flare produces intense deep inflammatory pain and mechanical restriction; the pain severity drives the urgency of the disposition (potent analgesia) rather than changing the diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Redness and heat confined to the lump area with no spreading erythema down the arm/chest and no red streaks

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Spreading bacterial cellulitis / secondary soft-tissue infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of rapidly advancing erythema, red streaking and warmth beyond the lesion makes an established spreading cellulitis unlikely now, though it remains a mandatory safety-net target telehealth cannot palpate for.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile (low-grade 37.4 only), no rigors, no systemic malaise; tachycardia attributable to pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, rigors and malaise (with tachycardia explained by severe pain) makes invasive/systemic infection unlikely now, though it remains a mandatory safety-net target.

symptoms.symptom_name: Multiple interconnected lesions in a chronically scarred/tunnelled area rather than a single new tense pointing boil; a discrete fluctuant collection cannot be excluded without palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The diffuse, interconnected, chronically scarred architecture argues against a simple discrete primary boil; however, whether one part has become a discrete drainable collection requires in-person palpation for fluctuance, which telehealth cannot perform — so it remains a mandatory reason for urgent in-person review.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lesions confined to flexural friction/skin-fold zones (axillae and groin); smoker

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A strictly flexural (axillae/groin) friction-zone distribution in a smoker fits the mechanical-friction, occlusion and smoking trigger profile of hidradenitis suppurativa.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic recurrent disease with high psychosocial burden, managed only with intermittent topical antibiotic (no systemic therapy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronicity, recurrence and marked quality-of-life/psychosocial impact are characteristic of hidradenitis suppurativa and signal the need for long-term specialist immunomodulatory management, not just acute treatment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (BP 135/85, HR 98, Temp 37.4, RR 18, SpO2 98%, Wt 92 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person measurement: afebrile with only a mildly raised heart rate (attributable to pain) and normal oxygen saturation argues against systemic sepsis. Not obtainable in telehealth without a home device.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent same-day in-person clinical assessment (GP / urgent care) with palpation of the axilla for a discrete fluctuant drainable collection and analgesia
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Palpation to distinguish/drain a discrete abscess from the diffuse flare, and delivery of potent analgesia, are the assessments telehealth cannot perform and are the immediate priority.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bacterial swab (microscopy, culture and sensitivities, including MRSA) of actively draining lesions if secondary infection is suspected or the flare is not settling
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Guides targeted antibacterial therapy where secondary infection is suspected; not required to make the clinical diagnosis of the flare.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Specialist dermatology referral for long-term disease control (assessment for immunomodulatory / biologic therapy)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Established Hurley II/III disease with recurrent flares and scarring needs specialist longitudinal management to prevent progression and further scarring.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Systemic bloods / blood cultures / septic screen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No systemic features (afebrile, no rigors, well) — a septic work-up would be over-investigation unless fever, rigors or systemic upset develop.
